lesson.title: Property & Tenancy Law
lesson.explanation: Rental Housing Act (1999): lease agreement should be written (verbal is valid but harder to prove). Deposit: landlord must invest in interest-bearing account, return with interest within 14 days of lease end. Inspections: joint inspection at move-in and move-out. Rent increases: reasonable, typically annually. Eviction: Prevention of Illegal Eviction Act (PIE) — court order required, no self-help evictions. Rental Housing Tribunal handles disputes (free service). Sectional Title: body corporate manages common property, levy contributions mandatory.

examples.problem: Practical scenario in Property & Tenancy Law

practice_problems.question: What is the first step when beginning work in Property & Tenancy Law?
Describe the key regulation or standard that governs Property & Tenancy Law in South Africa.
practice_problems.answer: See lesson content for specific SA regulations.
hints: Think about SANS standards or Acts
practice_problems.explanation: SA has specific standards for each trade.

misconceptions.misconception: You don't need formal training for Property & Tenancy Law
misconceptions.correction: Proper training and certification are required for safety and legal compliance in SA.
misconceptions.why_students_think_this: People see informal work and assume no qualifications are needed.
